verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific, gamete-restricted surface adhesion protein required for sperm-oocyte fusion. It is a single-pass transmembrane protein (Ig-like cell adhesion molecule family member) with an extracellular immunoglobulin domain. However, expression is strictly limited to the male germline (spermatozoa), making it a tissue-restricted surface protein. The protein does reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via its TM anchor and participates in cell-surface-mediated gamete binding and fusion — the surface biology is unambiguous — but the cellular context (germline-only expression) is sufficiently narrow that surface accessibility is restricted to a specialized developmental lineage rather than broadly accessible somatic cells. This maps to tissue_restricted_surface within the contextual verdict.
